statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input a singly linked list head node and an integer position. The list contains 1000 nodes. The values are integers 1 through 1000 (inclusive).
statement.outputFormat: Return the value at the given index in the list. If the index is out of bounds, return -1.
statement.notes: Use a custom LinkedList class and iterator.

hints.text: Check the implementation of the next function in the LinkedListIterator class.

editorial.title: LinkedListIterator
editorial.intuition: Implement a custom LinkedList class and an iterator that can be used to traverse the list.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force traversal of the list is not needed as we can implement an iterator.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a pointer to the current node in the LinkedListIterator class.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The iterator should be able to correctly traverse the list and return the value at the given index.
editorial.dryRun: Test the LinkedListIterator class with a sample list and verify its correctness.
commonMistakes: Incorrect implementation of the next function.
Not using a pointer to the current node in the iterator.
